data.title: Yields advance after strong economic data dents rate cut bets; US10Y reclaims 4.20%
data.description:
U.S. Treasury yields climbed on Thursday morning after strong economic data dented Federal Reserve rate cut expectations. Yields have generally been on an upswing amid an extended bond selloff since last week's decision by the central bank to cut interest rates for the first time in nearly nine months.

The Commerce Department revised second-quarter U.S. GDP growth higher to an annualized rate of 3.8% in its third estimate from 3.3%. Meanwhile, initial jobless claims data dropped for the second consecutive week. 

The two indicators pushed yields off their September lows, with the rate-sensitive U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) rising 5 basis points to 3.66%—its highest level since September 2. Meanwhile, the benchmark U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) added 4 basis points to 4.20%, the highest since September 4. 

See how yields trade across the curve here on Seeking Alpha’s bond page. 

“The U.S. economy is resilient and the strong GDP is another indication that we are not at risk of any kind of recession, even with slowing labor market growth,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated. 

Still, despite Thursday’s uptick, Treasury yields remain well below where they began the year. Year-to-date, the 2-year has dropped 60 basis points, while the 10-year has declined by 32 basis points.

Treasury ETFs: (NASDAQ:TLT), (NYSEARCA:TLH), (NASDAQ:IEF), (NASDAQ:IEI), (:SHY), (NYSEARCA:SGOV), (NYSEARCA:SCHO), and (NYSEARCA:BIL).

Bond ETFs: (NYSEARCA:AGG), (NASDAQ:BND), (NASDAQ:VCIT), (NYSEARCA:MUB), (NASDAQ:MBB), (NYSEARCA:JNK), (NYSEARCA:LQD), (NYSEARCA:HYG), and (NYSEARCA:TIP).

data.title: Metalero Mining announces $300,000 private placement to advance Benson project
data.description:
• Metalero Mining (TSXV:MLO:CA) announced a non-brokered private placement for gross proceeds of up to $300,000 to fund the next phase of work on its flagship Benson project, located in the prolific Quesnel Trough of central British Columbia.
  • The offering will consist of up to 1.25 million flow-through units at $0.24 per FT unit.
  • Each FT Unit will consist of one flow-through common share and one flow-through common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one additional FT share at $0.40 for one year from the date of issuance.
  • The proceeds will be used to support the Fall 2025 exploration work at Benson, including further sampling and ground geophysics.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Q/C Technologies begins trading under new ticker “QCLS”
data.description:
• Q/C Technologies (NASDAQ:QCLS) has officially changed its ticker symbol from “TNFA” to “QCLS”, effective today.
  • The rebranding reflects a strategic shift into quantum-class computing.
  • Q/C now holds exclusive global rights to LightSolver’s laser-based processing unit. The company will apply this technology to quantum-class cryptocurrency applications.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Planet Labs to build factory in Berlin, doubling production of Pelican satellites
data.description:
Planet Labs (NYSE:PL) on Thursday announced plans to establish a new satellite production facility in Berlin to meet rising demand for Earth observation and intelligence solutions in Europe. The investment, valued at more than €10 million ($11.7 million), is expected to create up to 70 jobs and expand the company’s Berlin workforce to about 220.

The new site will manufacture next-generation Pelican satellites, a high-resolution imaging system designed for persistent monitoring. Planet (NYSE:PL) already produces spacecraft at its San Francisco headquarters, where its Pelican production line is fully operational. The Berlin plant will double overall production capacity of the fleet, executives said.

“Europe needs its own eyes, and they need it now,” Will Marshall, co-founder and chief executive of Planet Labs (PL), said in the announcement. He cited Europe’s urgent need for autonomous intelligence capabilities, adding that the facility will enable faster deployment of satellites to support security and stability across the region.

German officials welcomed the investment as a sign of confidence in the country’s space sector. Dorothee Bär, federal minister of research, technology and space, said in the announcement that Planet’s (PL) expansion “clearly shows Germany’s attractiveness and competitiveness” in a strategically vital industry. Katherina Reiche, federal minister for economic affairs and energy, called the decision “a vote of confidence in Germany as a high-tech and space-industry hub” that would create skilled jobs and strengthen supply chains.

Martin Polak, managing director of Planet Labs (PL), said Berlin has long served as the company’s European headquarters, supporting global satellite operations and driving business across the EMEA region.

The expansion follows strong growth for Planet (PL) in Europe, including a €240 million multi-year agreement with the German government and a separate contract with NATO. Both deals highlight what the company described as a global imperative for enhanced daily monitoring, warning systems, and responsive space capabilities.

data.title: DeFi Technologies prices $100M equity offering
data.description:
• DeFi Technologies (NASDAQ:DEFT) has entered into a securities purchase agreement with several institutional investors, led by Galaxy Digital (GLXY).
  • Pursuant to the agreement, such investors have agreed to purchase 45,662,101 shares of common stock and warrants to purchase up to an additional 34,246,577 shares, at a combined purchase price of US$2.19 per share and three-quarters of one warrant.
  • The warrants will have an exercise price of US$2.63 per share, representing a 120% premium to the offering price, will be exercisable immediately upon issuance, and will expire 3 years from the date of issuance.
  • The gross proceeds to the company will be US$100M, net proceeds from which will be used for general corporate purposes, including funding working capital.
  • The offering is expected to close on September 26, 2025.
  • DEFT --16% premarket to $2.19
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BMO confident United Natural Foods can achieve profit targets despite downsizing
data.description:
BMO Capital Markets upgraded United Natural Foods (NYSE:UNFI) to Outperform from Market Perform as the company’s downsizing efforts coupled with successful customer retention gives analyst Kelly Bania confidence that the company can achieve its ambitious profitability targets.

The upgrade is fueling a 3% gain for UNFI in Thursday’s premarket trading.

The company’s network optimization strategy – which includes the closure of four distribution centers – has so far reduced square footage by 9% yet with stronger customer retention. This suggests UNFI can achieve its 3-year, $150M cost savings and EBITDA targets, Bania says.

Bania also mentions the company’s solid performance so far this year despite a cyber breach that hampered operations for three weeks in June.

“For FY25, we estimate underlying sales growth of 6% and EBITDA growth of 17%, which is solidly above management’s flat sales and highs-single-digit% EBITDA [compounded annual growth rate] three-year plan and despite UNFI’s modest mid-single-digit % YTD share price performance,” Bania writes in her note to clients.

In addition to an upgrade, BMO raised its target price for UNFI by 44% to $36, representing a 23% upside from Wednesday’s closing price, and FY26 EBITDA target to $640M from $615M versus $618M consensus estimates. FY26 sales target was raised to 0.9% from (1.6%) and FY27 to 2.6% from 1%.

data.title: Fed's Goolsbee says 'uneasy with front-loading too many rate cuts' based on just jobs data
data.description:
Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee on Thursday said that, even though he voted for the central bank's first interest rate cut in nearly nine months last week, he is "somewhat uneasy with front-loading too many rate cuts" based on just economic data showing a moderation in job growth. 

The Federal Open Market Committee on September 17 cut its key policy rate by 25 basis points, citing increased downside risks to the labor part of its dual mandate of stable consumer prices and maximum employment. 

"We're in this weird environment now where we're getting stagflation with direction shocks, which means things that are making both sides of the mandate worse at the same time. That's the worst scenario the central bank can be in, because there's not an obvious screwdriver solution to that," Goolsbee said in a moderated discussion in Grand Rapids, Mich.

"Now you're trying to balance which of these sides is getting worse quicker, which of these deteriorations is going to last longer. So, that's the context when we come into this last meeting. The job market seems to be cooling, and inflation is going up," the Chicago Fed President said. 

"I voted for the rate cut. I am somewhat uneasy with front-loading too many rate cuts based on the, just on the payroll, jobs numbers slowing down," he said. 

Fed Chair Jerome Powell last week called the central bank's rate cut a "risk management" move, dousing hopes that this would be the start of an extended monetary policy easing cycle. 

"If we get data that show we are on the path to maintain stable full employment and inflation is likely to keep coming back down to 2%, I think rates can go down a fair bit more from where they are even now, but I'm just a little uneasy with too much front-loading until we're sure that's happening," Goolsbee added on Thursday.

Uncertainties over the future of monetary policy have dented sentiment on Wall Street this week. Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU). 

More on the markets

data.title: CoreWeave expands contract with OpenAI by up to $6.5B
data.description:
CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) said it has expanded an agreement with OpenAI by up to $6.5B to power the training of the AI startup's most advanced next-generation models through its infrastructure.

Shares of CoreWeave dipped about 3% premarket on Thursday.

In March 2025, CoreWeave announced an initial agreement with OpenAI with a contract value of up to $11.9B, followed by an expanded agreement worth up to $4B in May.

The new agreement brings the total contract value with Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-backed OpenAI up to about $22.4B.

“CoreWeave has become an important partner in OpenAI’s broader infrastructure platform,” said Peter Hoeschele, vice president of Infrastructure and Industrial Compute.

CoreWeave is backed by Nvidia (NVDA).

Earlier this week, Nvidia (NVDA) said it intends to invest up to $100B in OpenAI progressively to build and deploy at least 10 gigawatts of AI data centers with Nvidia systems. The companies entered a strategic partnership to deploy at least 10 gigawatts of Nvidia systems — representing millions of GPUs — for OpenAI’s next-generation AI infrastructure to train and run its next generation of models.

data.title: OpenAI, Databricks unveil multi-year partnership to expand usage of OpenAI's models
data.description:
OpenAI and Databricks unveiled a new, multi-year, $100M partnership on Thursday to expand the usage of OpenAI's artificial intelligence models.

The deal will see OpenAI's models become natively available in Databricks Data Intelligence Platform and the Snowflake (NYSE:SNOW) competitor's flagship AI product, Agent Bricks. As such, OpenAI's models will be instantly available to all of Databricks customers across clouds. 

Among the capabilities that Databricks customers will have is the ability to bring OpenAI's models directly to their enterprise data, either via SQL or an API, to run the large language models at scale. It also gives Databricks customers the ability to have access to the latest OpenAI models.

“We're seeing overwhelming demand from enterprise customers looking to build AI apps and agents on their data, tailored to their unique business needs,” said Ali Ghodsi, Co-founder and CEO of Databricks in a statement. “This partnership makes it easier for enterprises to securely leverage their data and OpenAI models at scale with best-in-class governance and performance.”

OpenAI COO Brad Lightcap echoed those thoughts. “Enterprise demand for frontier AI is accelerating, and with Databricks, we're making its deployment even simpler without compromising the high bar for performance and production,” Lightcap explained. “Our partnership with Databricks brings our most advanced models to where secure enterprise data already lives, making it easier for businesses to experiment, deploy, and scale AI agents with real impact.”

Databricks announced last month that it had signed a term sheet for its Series K round, which values the company at $100B. OpenAI is financially backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), among other investors.

data.title: Banking sector holds steady but delinquencies pose risks, Apollo says
data.description:
Apollo Global Management’s chief economist Torsten Slok offered a measured outlook on the U.S. banking sector, pointing to both areas of resilience and looming challenges.

According to the asset management firm, overall bank balance sheets remain sound, supported by steady credit expansion. Much of this lending activity is being fueled by larger institutions, which continue to supply liquidity to businesses and households. 

Despite that strength, cracks are appearing in consumer credit. Delinquency rates on credit cards and auto loans have climbed, raising concerns about household financial stability. The resumption of student loan repayments is also expected to add pressure, weighing on both credit quality and lending momentum in the months ahead Slok noted. 

On the corporate side, the ongoing trade tensions have yet to show a material effect on credit growth or banking sector performance.

However, the impact of elevated interest rates is more apparent. Higher borrowing costs have dragged down valuations across commercial real estate, with office properties, multifamily housing, and healthcare facilities facing the sharpest declines. This dynamic is particularly worrisome for regional banks, which tend to hold greater exposure to the sector. 

Slok added that while the industry overall remains stable, lenders must navigate these headwinds carefully. 

Bank ETFs: (NASDAQ:KBWB), (NYSEARCA:KBE), and (NASDAQ:FTXO). 

Regional Bank ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KRE), (NASDAQ:KBWR), (NYSEARCA:IAT), and (NYSEARCA:DPST).

Financials ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XLF), (NYSEARCA:VFH), (NYSEARCA:IYF), (NYSEARCA:FNCL), (NYSEARCA:IYG), and (NYSEARCA:FXO).
